intent: Configure a static route on router 0 to 172.16.9.0/24 using the IP address of the neighbor connected to interface 0-eth2 as next-hop.
Configure a static route on router 2 to 172.16.7.0/24 using the IP of the device connected to 2-eth1 as next-hop.
Configure a backup static route on router 4 to 172.16.1.0/24 using the IP address of the peer of 4-eth0 as next-hop.
topology_resolution: 4-eth0 peers with 0-eth0 => neighbor IP = 10.0.0.1. No explicit distance given; 'backup' implies higher distance — treated as needs_human since no distance value is specified.
notes: The intent says 'backup' but does not provide an administrative distance value. A backup route requires a higher distance than the primary; the agent cannot infer the distance without additional context.

intent: Configure a static route on router 7 to 172.16.9.0/24 using the IP of the device linked to 7-eth1 as next-hop.
Configure a static route on router 1 to reach router 6 LAN using the IP of the neighbor attached to 1-eth0 as next-hop.
topology_resolution: 1-eth0 peers with 3-eth0 => neighbor IP = 10.0.3.2. Router 6 LAN = 172.16.6.0/24.

intent: Enable IPv6 forwarding on router 9 and add an IPv6 route to 3000::/64 through interface 9-eth0.
Bring up interface 8-eth0, assign the IPv4 address 10.0.8.10/30 to it, and then enable IPv4 forwarding on router 8.
Create a BGP peer-group named IBGP on router 0 and bind neighbor 10.0.1.2 to it.
notes: remote-as not specified in intent; needs_human because bgp_peer_group_config requires local_as and remote_as.

intent: Enable IPv4 forwarding on router 5 and set the MTU of interface 5-eth1 to 9216 bytes.
Bring interface 3-eth1 administratively up and configure its description as BACKBONE_TO_6.
Configure a static route on router 7 to 172.16.0.0/24 via 10.0.6.1 with administrative distance 50.
Set MED to 100 for BGP neighbor 10.0.2.2 and enable AS-path multipath on router 0.
notes: local_as not stated; BGP process on router 0 assumed to be 65000 based on topology context from B41.

expected_commands.command: vtysh -c 'configure terminal' -c 'router bgp 65000' -c 'neighbor 10.0.2.2 route-map SET_MED_OUT out'
vtysh -c 'configure terminal' -c 'router bgp 65000' -c 'bgp bestpath as-path multipath-relax'

intent: Configure a static route on router 3 to 172.16.4.0/24 via 10.0.4.2.
notes: 10.0.4.2 is router 4's 4-eth1. Router 3 has no direct link to 10.0.4.x — next-hop is not directly reachable from router 3 per topology.

intent: Configure BGP local-preference to 200 for peer-group EDGE on router 4.
notes: local_as not stated; assumed 65200 from B44 context where EDGE was created on router 4.

expected_commands.command: vtysh -c 'configure terminal' -c 'router bgp 65200' -c 'neighbor EDGE route-map SET_LOCALPREF in'

intent: Bring down interface 1-eth1 and remove its IP address.
Remove the static route pointing to 172.16.0.0/24 from router 7.
Disable ARP encapsulation on interface 0-eth2 of router 0.

expected_commands.command: ip link set dev 0-eth2 arp off

intent: Enable IPv4 forwarding globally on router 7.
Administratively shut down interface 8-eth0 on router 8.
Bind neighbor 10.0.8.2 to the BGP peer-group EDGE on router 4.
On router 9, add an IPv6 route to 3000::/64 through interface 9-eth0 and enable IPv6 forwarding.
Find the IP of the neighbor connected to interface 0-eth2, and configure a static route on router 0 to 172.16.9.0/24 via that IP.
On router 7, configure a static route to 172.16.0.0/24 via 10.0.6.1 with administrative distance 50.
Create a floating default route (0.0.0.0/0) on router 0 via 10.0.1.2 with administrative distance 250.
Configure a BGP peer-group named IBGP on router 0, set its remote-as to 65000, and bind neighbor 10.0.1.2 to it.

expected_commands.command: vtysh -c 'configure terminal' -c 'router bgp 65000' -c 'neighbor IBGP peer-group' -c 'neighbor IBGP remote-as 65000'

intent: Enable IP forwarding on router 7 and set the MTU of interface 7-eth1 to 9000 bytes.
On router 9, assign the IPv6 address 2001:db8:1::1/64 to interface 9-eth0.
Apply a rate limit of 100Mbps on interface 0-eth2 of router 0 specifically for outbound TCP traffic.

expected_commands.command: tc qdisc add dev 0-eth2 root handle 1: htb default 10
tc class add dev 0-eth2 parent 1: classid 1:1 htb rate 100mbit
tc filter add dev 0-eth2 parent 1: protocol ip u32 match ip protocol 6 0xff flowid 1:1

intent: Configure a static ARP entry on router 0 mapping IP 10.0.0.2 to MAC address 02:00:00:00:00:02.

expected_commands.command: ip neigh add 10.0.0.2 lladdr 02:00:00:00:00:02 dev 0-eth0 nud permanent

intent: Remove all static routes on router 7 that point to prefix 172.16.0.0/24.
Configure a static route on router 1 to reach the LAN subnet of router 5, using the IP of the neighbor connected to 1-eth1 as the gateway.
topology_resolution: 1-eth1 peers with 4-eth1 => neighbor IP = 10.0.4.2. Router 5 LAN = 172.16.5.0/24.

intent: Find the router connected to interface 0-eth1, and configure a static route on that remote router back to 172.16.0.0/24 via 10.0.1.1.
topology_resolution: 0-eth1 peers with 5-eth0 => remote router = 5. Router 0 LAN = 172.16.0.0/24. Next-hop 10.0.1.1 = router 0's 0-eth1 IP.

intent: Apply a 50Mbps rate limit for inbound UDP traffic on the specific interface of router 4 that connects to router 0.
topology_resolution: Router 4 interface connecting to router 0 = 4-eth0. UDP = IP protocol 17.

expected_commands.command: tc filter add dev 4-eth0 parent ffff: protocol ip u32 match ip protocol 17 0xff police rate 50mbit burst 10k drop flowid :1

intent: On router 2, create a BGP peer-group named TRANSIT, set remote-as 64512, and set the BGP authentication password to 'SeCrEt' for this group.
notes: BGP MD5 authentication password configuration is not supported in the current operation catalog.

intent: Set the local-preference to 150 for peer-group IBGP on router 0, and configure MED to 50 for neighbor 10.0.2.2.

expected_commands.command: vtysh -c 'configure terminal' -c 'router bgp 65000' -c 'neighbor IBGP route-map SET_LP in'
vtysh -c 'configure terminal' -c 'router bgp 65000' -c 'neighbor 10.0.2.2 route-map SET_MED_50 out'

intent: Apply a route-map named BLOCK_DEFAULT in the inbound direction for BGP neighbor 10.0.1.2 on router 0.

expected_commands.command: vtysh -c 'configure terminal' -c 'router bgp 65000' -c 'neighbor 10.0.1.2 route-map BLOCK_DEFAULT in'

intent: Remove the BGP peer-group EDGE from router 4 and delete all associated static routes pointing to 172.16.1.0/24.

expected_commands.command: vtysh -c 'configure terminal' -c 'router bgp 65200' -c 'no neighbor EDGE peer-group'

intent: Add a static route on router 6 to 172.16.8.0/24 via 10.0.6.2, and create a backup route to the exact same destination via 10.0.7.2 with administrative distance 200.
notes: 10.0.6.2 = router 7's 7-eth0. 10.0.7.2 = router 6's own 6-eth0 peer (router 3's 3-eth1 has 10.0.7.1, so 10.0.7.2 = 6-eth0 itself). Neither is a directly connected next-hop of router 6 — only 10.0.7.2 is on router 6's own subnet (6-eth0 = 10.0.7.2/30). Commands are generated as specified.

intent: Disable ARP, shut down interface 7-eth0, and remove its IPv4 address on router 7.

expected_commands.command: ip link set dev 7-eth0 arp off

intent: Enable dual-stack forwarding on router 3, then assign IPv4 10.0.3.1/30 and IPv6 2001:db8:3::1/64 to interface 3-eth0.
Change the IP address of 1-eth0 on router 1 to 10.0.10.1/30 and ensure the interface is administratively up.
Configure a blackhole route on router 9 for the prefix 10.100.0.0/16 by pointing it to the Null0 interface.
notes: FRR uses 'blackhole' keyword, not Null0 (Cisco syntax).

intent: Limit inbound ICMP traffic to 5Mbps on interface 2-eth0 of router 2.

expected_commands.command: tc filter add dev 2-eth0 parent ffff: protocol ip u32 match ip protocol 1 0xff police rate 5mbit burst 10k drop flowid :1

intent: Delete the static ARP entry for 10.0.0.2 on router 0, and clear the description of interface 0-eth0.
Disable IPv6 forwarding globally on router 4, but ensure IPv4 forwarding remains enabled.
Assign the IP address 10.255.255.1/32 to the loopback interface on router 1.
Set the description of interface 0-eth2 on router 0 to TO_ROUTER_9 and set the description of 9-eth0 on router 9 to TO_ROUTER_0.
Set the OSPF cost of interface 5-eth0 on router 5 to 100 and configure its description as PRIMARY_LINK.
Enable Jumbo frames by setting the MTU to 9216 on interface 8-eth0 of router 8, and restart the interface by bringing it down then up.
Find the IPv4 network CIDR assigned to interface 3-eth1 and configure a static route on router 0 pointing to that network via 10.0.1.2.
